title: deploy can log in over SSH, but nothing it runs actually executes
description_md:
The `deploy` account authenticates over SSH just fine -- its key works, no errors, no rejection. But every command it runs remotely (`ssh deploy@host '<command>'`) produces no output at all, as if the command were never actually run. SSH in as `trainee` (passwordless `sudo`), find what's silently overriding every command `deploy` tries to run, and fix it.

A test key is at `~/deploy_key`. You're done when `ssh -i ~/deploy_key deploy@localhost 'echo SSH_OK'` actually prints `SSH_OK`.
